occurrences.summary: CONTROLLED FIXTURE: expect sentinel "QA-DEMO-SENTINEL-8271" which is intentionally absent
The executive summary surface shows the shared readiness summary line (executiveSummary.readiness.summaryLine === unifiedReadiness.summaryLine).
The promoted card receives the subtle Deal highlight

review.sharedRootCause: Backend domain records and frontend read models evolved with similar but non-identical field names, while adapters spread raw records without an explicit versioned projection contract.
review.whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: Prior source-scan and service tests proved each side independently but did not assert the serialized Lead Workspace lifecycle field after an owner mutation.
review.whyBrowserQaMissedIt: Earlier journeys checked that components existed; they did not mutate a lifecycle state and then assert its semantic visual projection across refresh.
review.missingInvariant: Every domain lifecycle mutation must be projected through an explicit route field consumed by the UI, and route tests must assert both the stored value and serialized field.
review.missingAcceptanceJourney: A real-browser journey that creates a lead, promotes the same opportunity, asserts the Deal highlight, and repeats the assertion after refresh was missing.
review.sharedRepair: The Lead Workspace adapter now explicitly maps opportunity.lifecycle to lifecycleStatus instead of relying on raw-record field coincidence.
review.newRegressionTest: The Phase 1 route integration test asserts opportunity.lifecycleStatus on the serialized Lead Workspace and same-ID/publicUid promotion behavior.
review.newBrowserAssertion: phase1-manual-lead-promotion asserts deal-card-highlight immediately after pursuit and after hard refresh.

occurrences.summary: Spatial overlays are sampled against the mapped GIS geometry (1.15 ac). The card's own Acreage-basis disclosure states 'Overlays sampled against: gis geometry', so the FEMA flood coverage should be reported against 1.15 ac and never exceed it.
After repair, the FEMA flood overlay must be sampled against the ~1.15 ac mapped GIS geometry: a 100% zone should read ~1.15 ac and no overlay may report more acres than the mapped parcel (1.15 ac).

Every operator-visible flood-overlay surface must be sampled against the ~1.15 ac mapped GIS geometry; a 100% Zone-X overlay must read ~1.15 ac and no overlay may report more acres than the 1.15 ac mapped parcel anywhere on the card.

review.sharedRootCause: The flood overlay's operator-visible acreage is rendered from several independent surfaces — the FEMA finding's free-text flood.summary, report.reconciliation.flood, report.govDd.flood.note, the workStatus 'Flood overlay' item, and the floodCard detail — each of which embedded the acreage captured at research time, when the overlay area was sampled against a provider/assessed acreage (1.32) rather than the queried GIS geometry (1.15). The first repair only recomputed flood.zones[].approximateAcres and left every free-text surface unchanged, so the 100% Zone-X card still displayed 1.32 ac on a 1.15 ac parcel, now without even the overlay-exceeds-geometry warning (which had been silenced because floodOverlayAcres was forced to 1.15).
review.whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: checkOverlayConsistency and the zone-array normalization were unit tested, but no test asserted the FREE-TEXT flood surfaces (summary/reconciliation/govDd/workStatus) against the mapped geometry, and no test tied all flood-acreage surfaces to a single pinning helper. The partial fix passed its own tests while leaving the string surfaces wrong.
review.whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The first browser QA correctly caught the persisted 1.32 in the zone table; the partial repair moved the number in one field but not the summary/reconciliation strings, which only an adversarial recheck reading the actual rendered flood card and the /report payload surfaced.
review.missingInvariant: Every operator-visible flood/overlay acreage surface (summary, reconciliation.flood, govDd.flood.note, workStatus, decisionCard detail) MUST derive from the mapped GIS geometry via the single shared pinOverlayAcresToGeometry helper; no free-text surface may embed a persisted provider/assessed overlay acreage.
review.missingAcceptanceJourney: Read the rendered FEMA flood card text AND report.reconciliation.flood AND govDd.flood.note on the acceptance card and assert none states an acreage exceeding the mapped geometry (a 100% zone reads ~1.15 ac everywhere).
review.sharedRepair: One shared helper pinOverlayAcresToGeometry(zones, mappedAcres) in acreage-basis.ts feeds EVERY flood-acreage surface: (1) the operator record pins flood zones AND regenerates flood.summary (so floodCard detail + workStatus inherit it) on the /report path; (2) routes.ts projectPublicScreening pins zones before composing reconciliation.flood.primary and govDd.flood.note; (3) a new routes.ts pinRunOverlaysToGeometry(run) pins the flood task's zones+summary at read time for the /public-intelligence endpoint that feeds the DD Public Property Intelligence panel; and (4) public-property-intelligence-live samples overlay area against the queried geometry at the source for new runs. The recurrence lesson (a partial per-field fix) was resolved by routing ALL surfaces through the one helper across BOTH endpoints (/report and /public-intelligence).
review.newRegressionTest: src/landos/acreage-basis.test.ts 'pinOverlayAcresToGeometry (WS1 F1 regression)': 100% zone pins to mapped geometry, partial zone scales by percentage, null geometry leaves zones untouched, and a pinned zone never fails checkOverlayConsistency against the geometry.
review.newBrowserAssertion: On the acceptance card the FEMA flood card, report.reconciliation.flood.primary, and govDd.flood.note all state ~1.15 ac (never 1.32 ac) for a 100% Zone-X overlay, and no overlay exceeds the mapped parcel; verified across a hard refresh.

occurrences.summary: A material unresolved acreage basis (assessed 1.32 vs mapped 1.15) should surface a reconciliation issue in the reconciliation facts, consistent with the header/acreage-basis/value-readiness which all declare a CONFLICT and a Tyler decision.
A verified Deal Card presented to the operator should be internally consistent; the orchestration self-audit should pass (no 'need repair' state shown to Tyler).

The Acreage basis block presents the canonical WS1 acreage basis: assessed 1.32 ac vs mapped 1.15 ac, disputed=true, Tyler decision required, sources 'Pickens County assessor roll' / 'Pickens County GIS geometry' (official) — consistent with the acreage-conflict blockers shown elsewhere on the page.

review.failurePattern: reconciliation-ignores-acreage-conflict: a surface renders the legacy per-report acreage reconciliation (or its own derivation) instead of the canonical WS1 acreage basis, so a genuinely conflicted assessed-vs-mapped acreage presents as clean/reconciled while other surfaces on the same page disclose the conflict.
review.sharedRootCause: Two representations of acreage coexist: the legacy report.reconciliation record and the canonical acreage basis on operatorRecord.identity.acreageBasis. Consumers that reach for the legacy field (or guess a wrong property path and silently fall back to it, as the Lead Workspace read model did with input.operatorRecord.acreageBasis ?? report.reconciliation) reintroduce the pre-WS1 split-brain. The nullish-coalescing fallback hid the wrong path because the legacy record is always present.
review.whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: The read-model unit test supplied operatorRecord without a nested identity.acreageBasis and asserted only pass-through of other fields; no test asserted WHICH acreage source wins when both the canonical basis and the legacy reconciliation exist. Route integration tests asserted contract shape, not acreage-source precedence.
review.whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The scripted lead-workspace journey asserts roots, strategy count, quarantine, refresh, and viewport, but has no assertion that reconciles the rendered acreage basis against the identity record on the same page. Only the independent human-mandate QA cross-checked the acreage block against identity/blockers and caught the contradiction.
review.missingInvariant: Every operator surface that shows an acreage basis must render the canonical identity.acreageBasis whenever it exists; the legacy report.reconciliation may appear only when no canonical basis exists, and a disputed basis must never render with conflict:false anywhere on the page.
review.missingAcceptanceJourney: A conflicted-acreage Lead Workspace journey: open a lead whose canonical basis is disputed and assert the workspace acreage block discloses the dispute (both values, both sources, Tyler decision) and agrees with the API payload identity.acreageBasis.
review.sharedRepair: src/landos/lead-workspace.ts now composes identity.acreageBasis as the only primary source with the legacy record strictly as absent-canonical fallback; the workspace API payload therefore carries the same object at property.canonicalAcreage and property.identity.acreageBasis, making divergence structurally impossible for this consumer.
review.newRegressionTest: src/landos/lead-workspace.test.ts: 'renders the canonical identity acreage basis, never the legacy reconciliation, when both exist' and 'tolerates partial legacy data: legacy reconciliation appears only when no canonical basis exists'.
review.newBrowserAssertion: lead-workspace-acquisitions-readonly journey gained API/UI steps executed in a real browser on every run; the independent WS1 recheck reconciles property.canonicalAcreage against property.identity.acreageBasis on a genuinely conflicted lead (deal 19 acceptance case) and on a verified lead (deal 22).

occurrences.summary: A road whose ownership is unknown/unverified must be described as UNKNOWN ownership, not 'private', and the card must not assert that recorded private-road access rights are required unless the road is actually classified private. The public-intel road task already uses the correct wording: both roads '(non-public)', with contact/ROW/frontage/physical/legal/maintenance separated.

After the F6 repair, NO surface of the access record may call an unknown-ownership road 'private' or assert that recorded private-road access rights are required unconditionally. accessStatus.concerns must be consistent with the fixed summary/decision card (unknown ownership; recorded private-road rights only conditional on the road being confirmed private).

review.sharedRootCause: A non-public / TIGER road-TYPE tag (road.owner matching /private/) was treated as established private OWNERSHIP. This produced two operator-visible surfaces that called an unknown-ownership road 'private' and asserted recorded private-road rights are required: (1) computeAccessStatus's decision-card summary, and (2) a free-text concern string emitted at the source (public-property-intelligence-live) and carried verbatim through accessStatus.concerns. The first repair fixed only the summary/decision-card path and left the concerns-array string, which is still served in the canonical /report operatorRecord.
review.whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: Tests asserted accessStatus.status and (after the first repair) the summary wording, but never asserted the accessStatus.concerns array text, and no test ran the shared sanitizeAccessLanguage over the persisted private-road concern string. The offending text lived in a field no test inspected.
review.whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The frontend FeasibilityStrip renders only accessStatus.summary + unresolved, not concerns, so the residual is not on the rendered deal card today — only an adversarial reviewer reading the raw /report payload caught it. It is one render/export away from the operator, so it is a real defect in the canonical record.
review.missingInvariant: No operator-record field (summary, concerns, workStatus, or any evidence string) may assert a road IS private or that recorded private-road rights ARE required from a road-layer/type tag; ownership is 'unknown' until proven, and the recorded-rights requirement is conditional. The shared sanitizeAccessLanguage enforces this on every persisted-run read/write.
review.missingAcceptanceJourney: Assert the FULL accessStatus object (summary AND concerns) plus the served /report operatorRecord.accessStatus.concerns[] contain no unconditional 'private road' / 'recorded access rights are required' assertion for an unknown-ownership road.
review.sharedRepair: Extended the shared sanitizeAccessLanguage (which runs at read AND write time on every persisted public-intelligence run) to rewrite 'is mapped as a private road' -> 'carries a non-public/private tag in the road layer, but ownership is unverified' and 'recorded access rights over it are required (and are not confirmed by GIS)' -> 'recorded access rights would be required only if it is confirmed private (unconfirmed by GIS)'. computeAccessStatus already maps every concern through this sanitizer, so all surfaces (summary + concerns) are covered. Also fixed the source guard in public-property-intelligence-live to emit the conditional unknown-ownership wording for new runs.
review.newRegressionTest: evidence-language.test.ts 'never asserts an unknown-ownership road is private or that recorded rights are required (ws2-r5)' asserts the sanitizer neutralizes the legacy private-road concern; operator-property-record.test.ts asserts computeAccessStatus summary uses unknown-ownership language and de-duplicates concerns.
review.newBrowserAssertion: On the acceptance card, neither the rendered Road/Access card NOR the served /report operatorRecord.accessStatus.concerns[] asserts the road is private or that recorded private-road rights are required; ownership reads unknown/unverified with a conditional rights clause.

occurrences.summary: Every report consumes the same shared readiness record: all five strategies blocked / not scoreable / not actionable, pricing gate closed, value conflicted, comp counts from the validated unique registry (55 sold / 51 active).
No surface calls a bare median 'the valuation basis' while the shared record states a computable median is preliminary context only and value readiness is conflicted.
Every remaining-verification item names the fact needing confirmation.
Each blocker names a distinct fact once; the same acreage fact is not repeated in variant wording.
Comp rows in an operator-facing report come from the deduplicated unique registry; no property appears twice.
The Lead Workspace opens (same surface as /dept/acquisitions?deal=19); no silent fallback to the quarantined legacy Deal Card.
Header reads 'LEAD WORKSPACE · V1.0' (clean middle dot).

The workspace reflects the recorded resolution outcome: an attempt ran and returned state 'unresolved' with an explanatory basis (geocode-only, confidence 0.3, per landos_resolution_snapshot / landos_parcel_identity), preserving that provenance for the operator.

identityConflict recorded (requested 4180-07-78-9999 vs resolved 4180-07-78-1710); WRONG PARCEL - HARD STOP banner (data-testid lead-workspace-conflict) naming both APNs; chip BLOCKED - WRONG PARCEL; not verified; no property intelligence, valuation, offer range, actionable strategies, or seller brief. The engine unit test (property-resolution-engine.test.ts:88) expects this for requested-vs-resolved APN mismatch.
Hard stop for the address/APN contradiction; at minimum no mutation of existing deal 19 records without Tyler confirmation.
A verified lead Resolution box presents the verification provenance, or clearly separates the historical geocode-era snapshot from the current verified state.
Rendered rows show normalized $/acre (they do) and the validated active listings advertised by the counts line (51 validated active) are reachable in the UI.
The Phase 1 organization surface uses the approved functional labels, including Acquisitions Agent and Property Research Agent, and contains no mascot labels.
Promotion remains visibly highlighted after refresh
The persisted discovery package is visible after managed restart
The repaired navigation path remains healthy after managed restart.
The journey should confirm that the board renders opportunity cards without requiring exactly one total card.
The independent journey should recognize the visible business-stage lanes.
The durable investigative mission survives the canonical managed restart and remains visible in the live browser.
